_doc: Canonical card catalogue for the CDiscourse UX/UI Roadmap GitHub Project (#1 under kyleruff1). Only contains the QOL-NNN cards that are NOT already covered by an existing TL/VG/BR/SC/ST/EV/SW/IX/PR/HOST/GAL/RULE/AN/PM issue. Source of truth for syncUxProjectBoard.js (dry-run) and applyUxProjectBoard.sh (operator-runnable). Do not put any secret values here. Body fields render verbatim into GitHub issue bodies. Field options are constrained to the live project schema captured 2026-05-18: Priority P0/P1/P2 (no P3), Effort S/M/L/XL (no XS), Release 6.5/6.6/6.7/6.8 (no 6.9), Epic from the existing 14, Phase Backlog/Design/Build/Review/Done/Blocked. Status defaults to Todo on creation.

supersededByExisting.QOL-001: #1 TL-001 Make Timeline the default room landing mode
supersededByExisting.QOL-003: #9 SC-001 Consolidate controls into the side action rail
supersededByExisting.QOL-011: #25 PR-003 Avatar upload policy and storage
supersededByExisting.QOL-012: #27 HOST-001 Dev hosting architecture for cdiscourse.com/dev
supersededByExisting.QOL-013: #28 HOST-002 Dev environment banner and safety boundary
supersededByExisting.QOL-014: #29 HOST-003 Deployment smoke checklist + #35 AN-002 Visual QA snapshots

cards.title: QOL-015 - Admin email delivery validation (mock first, live operator-gated)
body: ## Goal
Validate the `request-argument-deletion` admin notification path end-to-end before public dev hosting goes live.
## Acceptance
- Local/mock test path proves the Edge Function builds the admin notification payload correctly without sending a real email.
- Resend `not_configured` fallback is exercised in tests and returns a non-secret response.
- Test asserts Authorization header, RESEND key, and admin email addresses are never echoed back to the client.
- Dry-run path documented in `docs/admin-email-validation-plan.md`.
- Live email send remains operator-gated; this card does NOT authorise a live send.
## Out of scope
- Sending real emails to real admin inboxes (deferred to QOL-016 operator step).
- Adding new admin notification surfaces.
## Non-negotiables
- No service-role usage from client code.
- No raw admin email addresses printed in test fixtures, snapshots, or commit messages.

cards.title: QOL-016 - Supabase Auth email + redirect settings audit for cdiscourse.com/dev
body: ## Goal
Audit Supabase Auth email settings (signup confirmation, password reset, magic link, email change, invite) against the cdiscourse.com/dev hosting target before public dev deployment.
## Acceptance
- Checklist enumerates every Auth email template, its sender, its subject, its redirect URL.
- All redirect URLs include cdiscourse.com/dev (or whichever URL HOST-001 lands on) and exclude localhost from production allow-list.
- Local docs note required Supabase project settings (Auth -> URL Configuration, Auth -> Email Templates).
- No live email send by this card.
- No secret values written to docs.
## Depends on
- HOST-001 chooses final URL shape (cdiscourse.com/dev vs dev.cdiscourse.com).
- QOL-015 admin notification path proven on the mock side first.

cards.title: QOL-017 - GitHub Projects automation script (sync roadmap to Project #1)
body: ## Goal

Keep `docs/ux-ui-project-board.md`, repo issues, and Project #1 in sync via a small, safe, dry-run-default script. Avoid hand-editing 35+ project items in the GitHub UI when a doc/issue rename or rebucket happens.
## Acceptance
- `scripts/github/syncUxProjectBoard.js` exists, defaults to `--dry-run`, only mutates GitHub when `--apply` is passed AND `gh` is installed AND `gh auth status` is clean with `project` scope.
- `scripts/github/uxBoardCards.json` is the single source-of-truth catalogue with field options validated against the live project schema.
- `scripts/github/applyUxProjectBoard.sh` is the operator-runnable equivalent (Bash-only, no Node deps) for environments where Node isn't available.
- npm scripts `github:ux-board:dry` and `github:ux-board:apply` wired up.
- Tests cover catalogue validation, prefix dedupe, secret-shape rejection, and dry-run-no-network.
- Script never prints `gh` tokens, Bearer tokens, or any `Authorization:` header value.
## Status

Initial scaffold landed in this commit. Future iteration: add `--apply` path that actually issues `gh issue create / project item-add / project item-edit` calls deterministically, not just delegates to the bash script.

cards.title: QOL-018 - Repo-local Claude agent charters for CDiscourse
body: ## Goal

Define repo-local charters for the recurring agent roles future Claude Code sessions take on (project-board-manager, ux-board-designer, timeline-gameboard-implementer, sidecar-tools-implementer, supabase-email-validation-tester, bot-corpus-runner, qa-secret-scan-verifier).
## Acceptance
- `docs/agent-charters.md` enumerates each role with mission, allowed tools, forbidden actions, files to read first, verification commands, and final-report format.
- Charters are documentation only — they do NOT create `.claude/agents/*.md` files unless the repo establishes that convention later.
- Bot skill SKILL.md files under `.claude/skills/bot-provocateur/` and `.claude/skills/bot-revocateur/` are unchanged (validator hashes must match).
- `npm run skills:validate` continues to pass.
## Why not .claude/agents

- Charters are role guidance, not executable subagent definitions. The skills convention (`disable-model-invocation: true`, manual operator-only) intentionally rejects model-spawned bot behaviour. Charters preserve that boundary while still giving future sessions clear scope.

cards.title: QOL-019 - Bot tester prompt refresh: more dynamic, still safe
body: ## Goal

Make bot-fixture prompts (open-room engagement + existing-room engagement + xAI adversarial thread continuation) more dynamic and lifelike while preserving the existing safety contract (no persona pretends to be a real human, no canned phrases, structured quote + axis + mechanism + evidenceDebt pressure).
## Acceptance
- Prompt variants pull from a wider lexicon without introducing forbidden tokens (`troll`, `bot`, `astroturfer`, etc. — see anti-amplification doctrine).
- Anti-axis-repeat doctrine continues to rotate disagreement axes inside a single room.
- Heat-band escalation copy varies but never claims correctness/truth/winner.
- Some dev/test rooms reach HOT band naturally inside `bot:engage:open:tiny` and `bot:engage:existing:pilot`.
- No live run by this card; tests + dry-run only.
- Operator approval still required for any live `--pilot` invocation.
## Non-negotiables
- No raw hostile text from xAI source-harvest input is rendered verbatim into a generated move.
- No new external AI provider added.

cards.title: QOL-020 - Open-room engagement runner patch: filter, side-respect, coverage targeting
body: ## Goal
Tighten `scripts/bot-fixtures/runOpenRoomEngagementBots.js` and `scripts/bot-fixtures/engageExistingRooms.js` against three classes of issue surfaced by the 2026-05-18 dry/live runs.
## Acceptance
- Rooms with `moveCount < 1` are skipped (no bot enters an empty room with no opening claim).
- When a bot user is already a participant on a side, the runner respects that existing side and does not re-enrol on the opposite side.
- Coverage targeting hits 33-66% of eligible open rooms per run, deterministic given the same seed.

- `--dry` mode still emits the full event set (`run_start`, `room_candidate`, `bot_assignment`, `move_prompt_built`, `move_rendered`, `move_validated`, `submit_attempt`, `submit_result`, `room_heat_update`, `room_summary`, `run_summary`).
- No service-role usage. No direct insert into `public.arguments`. Posts go only through `submit-argument`.
- Operator approval gate intact for `--pilot`.
